lessonTitle: Unit 7: Section 3: Psychosis  ..............................................................................    22: identify some of the important treatments for psychotic behaviour

targetOutcomes.title: identify some of the important treatments for psychotic behaviour

prerequisiteKnowledge: distinguish the main kinds of psychosis
misconceptions: Students may memorize terms without recognizing examples, counterexamples, or application contexts.
Students may use Psychosis as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.

sourceReferences.exampleSnippet:
CASE #1
• serious psychosis involving loss of contact with reality
• delusions; hearing voices
•   loss of feeling, emotionally flat
• longing for protection and dependency
• social withdrawal
• sensitive to stimuli
Diagnosis:  ________________________________________
©Thinkstock

24
Overview
General Psychology 20
CASE #2
• false reasoning
• hallucinations and possibility of three types of delusions
• defensive, resentful
•     hypersensitive
Diagnosis:  ________________________________________
CASE #3
• exaggerated moods
• highs with constant motion and non-stop talking
• lows with feelings of sadness, no energy and sleep disruptions
• turns hostility inward
• high goals with feelings of worthlessness if they are not achieved
Diagnosis:  ___________________________________________
CASE #4
• lack of connection to a sense of identity
• triggered by acute physical and emotional pain in an atmosphere of helplessness
• fusion of the fragmented parts is an important goal
Diagnosis:  ____________________________________________
CASE #5
• anger turned inward
• harm to self (physical pain reduces emotional pain)
• numb, out of touch with life
• cannot express emotional needs, inability to tolerate intense feelings, mood
swings
Diagnosis:  ___________________________________________
CASE #6
• a  large group of illnesses whereby damage or disease to the body affects one’s
mental state, especially situations such as
◦   serious substance abuse over an extended time
◦   a decline in intellectual ability from Alzheimer’s or strokes
Diagnosis:  _____________________________________________

General Psychology 20
